(born 1965 in [Boulogne-Billancourt](/wiki/Boulogne-Billancourt "Boulogne-Billancourt")) is a modern French composer.
Craig M. Wright *The maze and the warrior: symbols in architecture, theology, and music* p. 325
["*A Circle in the Sand* (2001)"](https://brahms.ircam.fr/search_result?search=%27%27A+Circle+in+the+Sand%27%27+%282001%29) (work details) (in French and English). [IRCAM](/wiki/IRCAM "IRCAM").

From Wikipedia, the free encyclopedia

(Redirected from [Thierry Pecou](/w/index.php?title=Thierry_Pecou&redirect=no "Thierry Pecou"))

French composer (born 1965)

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/b/b8/Thierry_pecou.jpg/250px-Thierry_pecou.jpg)](/wiki/File:Thierry_pecou.jpg)

Thierry Pécou

**Thierry Pécou** (born 1965 in [Boulogne-Billancourt](/wiki/Boulogne-Billancourt "Boulogne-Billancourt")) is a modern French composer.

## Works

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Thierry_P%C3%A9cou&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Works")]

* *L'homme armé*[[1]](#cite_note-1)
* *Le Tombeau de [Marc-Antoine Charpentier](/wiki/Marc-Antoine_Charpentier "Marc-Antoine Charpentier")* pour 3 chœurs à voix égales, orgue baroque, basse de viole, positif et cloches (1995) [2]
* *A Circle in the Sand* (2001) for solo violin and choir, commissioned and premiered by Madeleine Mitchell (violin) and the Joyful Company of Singers with funding from Arts Council England, at the Bath International Music Festival July 2001[[2]](#cite_note-2)
* *L'Oiseau innumérable* – recording by [Alexandre Tharaud](/wiki/Alexandre_Tharaud "Alexandre Tharaud") and the [Ensemble Orchestral de Paris](/wiki/Ensemble_Orchestral_de_Paris "Ensemble Orchestral de Paris"), dir. [Andrea Quinn](/wiki/Andrea_Quinn "Andrea Quinn"), on [Harmonia Mundi](/wiki/Harmonia_Mundi "Harmonia Mundi")
* *Symphonie du Jaguar* – Ensemble Zellig's recording on [Harmonia Mundi](/wiki/Harmonia_Mundi "Harmonia Mundi") was awarded the [Diapason d'Or](/wiki/Diapason_d%27Or "Diapason d'Or") 2010 in the contemporary category.

## References

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Thierry_P%C3%A9cou&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: References")]

1. **[^](#cite_ref-1)** Craig M. Wright *The maze and the warrior: symbols in architecture, theology, and music* p. 325
2. **[^](#cite_ref-2)** ["*A Circle in the Sand* (2001)"](https://brahms.ircam.fr/search_result?search=%27%27A+Circle+in+the+Sand%27%27+%282001%29) (work details) (in French and English). [IRCAM](/wiki/IRCAM "IRCAM").
